calendar.matters:
Petition For Order
Off Calendar Dec-01-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ron Peoples, via ZOOM, for Linda Bell, Petitioner; Nicholas Klingenberg, via ZOOM, for John Bell and Michael Bell, Interested Parties.

The court asks why notice in the form of a summons has not been given to Michael Bell and John Bell. Mr. Klingenberg reports that he has agreed to waive notice and the court agrees to waive the defect in notice in open court. The court then points Mr. Peoples to the issue of venue. Mr. Peoples reports that venue is proper because the principal location of administration is his office. The court points Mr. Peoples to probate codes 17000 and 17002 and that the determination of proper venue falls under the code. Mr. Klingenberg states that the parties are working to resolve this matter and that a provision of the agreement is that he agree to this being the proper venue. The court points to the fact that Mr. People's client lives in Oceanside and the other trustees live in Daly City. After additional discussion, the court advises the parties that the court's tentative is to determine whether venue is proper and dismiss the petition without prejudice. Mr. Klingenberg suggests continuing this matter to allow the parties to resolve this matter. Mr. Peoples asks that if the court does not deem the venue proper, that the court allow for the matter to be transferred to the correct venue in lieu of dismissing the case, to avoid additional delays. The court advises counsel that the court intends to rule only on the venue aspect of the petition at this time.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Order is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
